bio: Hi, I'm Ming-Han Lee, a Research Engineer specializing in the intersection of Machine Learning, Distributed Systems, and Robotics. I earned my Master's degree from ETH Zurich, where my work focused on the architectural synergy between Machine Learning, Systems, and Learning-Based Robotic Control. My core research interest lies in bridging the gap between high-level algorithms and low-level system performance, ensuring that complex intelligence can thrive within the millisecond-level constraints of real-world robotics.

Professionally, I interned at the Robotics and AI Institute (RAI), where I advanced research in model-based reinforcement learning while completing my master's thesis. I also served at Robotic Systems Lab, specializing in re-architecting frontier model pipelines and successfully deploying high-performance algorithms under rigorous real-time constraints.

Outside the lab, I'm a completionist gamer with a deep appreciation for world-building. I'm driven by the challenge of transforming digital inspiration into tangible reality, turning what was once "only fantasy" into the future we inhabit.
